title: Governance Rule
description: A machine-enforceable governance rule applied to an API artifact (OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, JSON Schema, traffic, configuration) by a linter, registry, gateway, or runtime.

id.description: Unique, stable identifier for the rule (kebab-case).

name.description: Short human-readable name for the rule.

description.description: Full description of what the rule checks and why.

message.description: Message shown when the rule is violated.

rationale.description: Business or engineering reason the rule exists.

scope.description: Governance scope the rule belongs to.

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle stage at which the rule is enforced.

given.description: JSONPath, JMESPath, or selector locating the value the rule applies to.

then.description: Action taken when the rule matches.

function.description: Name of the function the rule invokes (e.g. truthy, falsy, pattern, length).

conformance.description: RFC 2119 requirement level the rule expresses.

tags.description: Domain tags for grouping and discovery.

guidanceUrl.description: Link to longer-form guidance backing the rule.

owner.description: Person or governance body accountable for the rule.

status.description: Lifecycle status of the rule itself.
